institution: Faculty of Engineering, Royal University of Phnom Penh (FE-RUPP)

grading_scale.note: Assessment performed at end of each semester. Shown as GPA and Cumulative GPA on 4.00 scale.

honor_bachelor_programs.name: Food Technology And Engineering
honor_bachelor_programs.overview: The bachelor program of Engineering in Food Technology and Engineering was established in February 2022. The Program focuses on the most needed food technologist and engineer in food industry to support and push forward Cambodia's development on agro-industry sustainably. The program addresses gaps in knowledge and skills and promotes high commitment to the development of agro-industry and food security.
honor_bachelor_programs.description: Developed with the participation of experts and researchers from Osaka Prefecture University. Designed to provide students with the knowledge, skills, and experience necessary for a successful career in food technology in Cambodia as well as in Southeast Asia. Covers food science, food processing technology, food safety, nutrition engineering, and sustainability in food systems.
last_semester_optional_tracks: Coursework - for students who would like to conduct coursework and final project
Research Thesis - for outstanding students who would like to conduct their research project under the supervision of BE faculty members
Extended Training - for students who would like to participate in a long training period (for the entire semester) under a co-operative training program with companies or organizations
honor_bachelor_programs.internship_requirement: Students are required to conduct a minimum 6 weeks internship at a standard company or organization during their vacation time of the third year or before.

curriculum.note: L = Lecture, P = Practice, S = Self Study

semester_1.subject: English Composition: Critical thinking, reading and writing
Sociology: Digital skill and Problem Solving
Introduction to Food Technology (Concept of engineering and technology)
Chemistry and Physical for Food Analysis

semester_2.subject: Design and Experimental Planning Food Science
Chemistry and Technology of Milk and Dairy Products
Startup Business with Food Science and Technology

semester_1.subject: Principle of Quality Assurance and Quality Control in Food

job_prospects: QA and QC Specialist / Food Safety Specialist / Laboratory Manager

honor_bachelor_programs.name: Data Science And Engineering
honor_bachelor_programs.overview: Bachelor of Data Science and Engineering program at Faculty of Engineering, Royal University of Phnom Penh is intended to give students a top-notch education in the art of extracting value from data assets so that we can better understand the past, manage the present, and effectively plan for the future. Graduates with expertise in this field are in high demand since it is essential to advancing industry, commerce, governance, and research.
honor_bachelor_programs.description: During the program, students are required to do practicum from year 2 to year 4 with real world problems. Doing practicum plays a crucial role in training students to acquire valuable experience, required skill sets and ability to be ready for work after graduation. The curriculum is designed for students with abilities to explore data and discover its potential — how to find innovative solutions to real problems in science, business and government. Students will learn Business Analysis and Machine Learning (ML) / Artificial Intelligence (AI) skills in addition to data management, analytics, and processing skills.
last_semester_optional_tracks: Coursework - for students who would like to conduct coursework and final project
Research Thesis - for outstanding students who would like to conduct their research project under the supervision of BE faculty members
Extended Training - for students who would like to participate in a long training period (for the entire semester) under a co-operative training program with companies or organizations
honor_bachelor_programs.internship_requirement: Students are required to conduct a minimum 6 weeks internship at a standard company or organization during their vacation time of the third year or before.

curriculum.note: L = Lecture, P = Practice, S = Self Study

semester_2.subject: English: Introduction to Technical and Business Writing
Introduction to Data Science and Engineering

semester_1.subject: Introduction to Big Data and Web Mining

extended_training_track.subject: Extended Training (full semester cooperative program with company)

honor_bachelor_programs.name: Automation And Supply Chain System Engineering
partnerships: Sirindhorn International Institute of Technology (SIIT), Thammasat University (TU), Thailand
honor_bachelor_programs.overview: Automation and Supply Chain Systems Engineering (ASCSE) was established in 2022 to deal with automation and digital manufacturing. The supply chain systems engineering is introduced to modernize manufacturing processes and optimize the whole supply chain systems. Established to comply with the rectangular strategies of the Royal Government of Cambodia (RGC) to develop human resources and digitalize manufacturing toward Industry 4.0.
honor_bachelor_programs.description: Features a 2+2 Bachelor's degree (International Program) with SIIT, Thammasat University, Thailand. Students study Years 1 and 2 at ASCSE, RUPP, Cambodia, then choose to continue Years 3 and 4 at RUPP or transfer credits to study at SIIT, TU, Thailand. Designed to build students' foundation in designing, operating, evaluating, and improving automation and supply chain systems while enhancing analytical and problem-solving skills.
honor_bachelor_programs.program_2plus2_note: Students can choose to study Years 3 and 4 at RUPP or transfer credits to study at the School of Management Technology (MT), SIIT, TU, Thailand.
honor_bachelor_programs.department_vision: To enhance and advance students' knowledge, skills, and leadership in the field of automation and supply chain systems so that they are capable of integrating and ready to work in regional and global markets.
department_mission: To produce high qualified undergraduate students who have comprehensive, advanced, and up-to-date knowledge for analytic and problem-solving skills
To enhance students' capabilities in designing, operating, evaluating, and improving automation and supply chain systems in manufacturing and service industries
To empower students to integrate technologies to identify bottlenecks of and propose solutions for local SMEs
To promote academic and industrial collaboration (e.g., internship and exchange programs) with local and international partners
To be a leading international department in the field of automation and supply chain systems
degree_requirements: Finish an internship at the industry in the third year and second semester
Finish writing their thesis and successfully defend in front of supervisor and committee members in the fourth year
Finish all 168 credits within the limited period
Complete bachelor's degree within five years
Pass all course requirements with 50% of total score
last_semester_optional_tracks: Coursework - for students who would like to conduct coursework and final project
Research Thesis - for outstanding students who would like to conduct their research project under the supervision of BE faculty members
Extended Training - for students who would like to participate in a long training period (for the entire semester) under a co-operative training program with companies or organizations
honor_bachelor_programs.internship_requirement: Students are required to conduct a minimum 6 weeks internship at a standard company or organization during their vacation time of the third year or before.

curriculum.note: L = Lecture, P = Practice, S = Self Study

semester_1.subject: Introduction of Algorithms and Object-oriented Programming

semester_2.subject: Statistics for Data Science and Empirical Study
Fundamentals of Financial and Managerial Accounting
Kinematics and Dynamics of Machine Elements and Mechanisms
Seminar with Logistics and Supply Chain Management

honor_bachelor_programs.name: Environmental Engineering
partnerships: Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology (GIST), Republic of Korea
honor_bachelor_programs.overview: The Department of Environmental Engineering provides an Honor Bachelor Program designed for exceptionally motivated and high-achieving students who want thorough, in-depth education about environmental challenges and solutions. With support from KOICA and GIST (Republic of Korea), the Department educates students to understand interactions between human actions and environmental effects and develop the capability to identify and address environmental challenges.
honor_bachelor_programs.description: Curriculum based on four-year coursework. Students learn from professors and researchers based in Cambodia and the Republic of Korea about knowledge and skills to minimize human exposure to pollutants and waste and protect the environment from adverse/hazardous impacts. Covers approaches and techniques in air/climate, water, soil/ecosystems and energy/resources.
last_semester_optional_tracks: Coursework - for students who would like to conduct coursework and final project
Research Thesis - for outstanding students who would like to conduct their research project under the supervision of faculty members

curriculum.note: L = Lecture, P = Practice, S = Self Study

semester_2.subject: Computer Applications and Software in Environmental Engineering
Unit Operations and Process for Environmental Engineering 1

semester_1.subject: Unit Operations and Process for Environmental Engineering II

bachelor_programs.name: Bio Engineering Biotechnology
bachelor_programs.overview: Bio-Engineering/Biotechnology is a branch of engineering that deals with chemical and biological processes used to develop and make products and practices used in Medical and Health application, Food and Agro industry, Agriculture and Environment. The areas of study are quite diverse.
bachelor_programs.description: The curriculum provides fundamental knowledge in several major study areas so that students will be well prepared for work in biotechnical field such as food industry, microbial and enzyme biotechnology industry, gene technology, plant cell technology, either in industrial factories or in public and private research institutes. The program prepares engineers for life-long achievement through education in the principles of Bio-Engineering/Biotechnology and development of communication, teamwork, leadership and entrepreneurship skills.
last_semester_optional_tracks: Coursework - for students who would like to conduct coursework and final project
Research Thesis - for outstanding students who would like to conduct their research project under the supervision of BE faculty members
Extended Training - for students who would like to participate in a long training period (for the entire semester) under a co-operative training program with companies or organizations
bachelor_programs.internship_requirement: Students are required to conduct a minimum 6 weeks internship at a standard company or organization during their vacation time of the third year or before.

semester_1.subject: Unit Operation for Food Engineering I

semester_2.subject: Unit Operation for Food Engineer II

bachelor_programs.name: Information Technology Engineering
bachelor_programs.overview: Bachelor of Information Technology Engineering (ITE) program at Faculty of Engineering, Royal University of Phnom Penh is designed to provide high-quality training in Information Technology which meets the current and future labor market requirements by emphasizing a broad foundation of computer organization, software, and mathematics both theoretical and practical aspects.
bachelor_programs.description: With elective courses such as Artificial Intelligence, Computer Graphics, Game Development, Data Mining, and Security, it enables students to develop diverse industrial applications. Students are required to do practicum from year 2 to year 4 with real world problems. Doing practicum plays a crucial role in training students to acquire valuable experience and skill sets. Lecturers and Researchers of ITE department mostly graduated from abroad. Key success is giving more homework/assignment/project to students to build real products.
last_semester_optional_tracks: Coursework - for students who would like to conduct coursework and final project
Research Thesis - for outstanding students (GPA > 3.5) who would like to conduct their research project under the supervision of BE faculty members
Extended Training - for students who would like to participate in a long training period (for the entire semester) under a co-operative training program with companies or organizations
bachelor_programs.internship_requirement: Students are required to conduct a minimum 6 weeks internship at a standard company or organization during their vacation time of the third year or before.

curriculum.note: L = Lecture, P = Practice, S = Self Study

semester_1.subject: Web Technologies and Cloud Computing I

bachelor_programs.name: Telecommunication & Electronics Engineering
bachelor_programs.overview: Telecommunication and Electronics Engineering integrates two technologies: Telecommunication technology and Electrical technology. Telecommunication technology covers communication over long distance via cable, electromagnetic wave (wireless) or light signal. Electronics technology focuses on design of integrated circuits with diodes, transistors, resistors, capacitors, and sensors to control electron flow.
bachelor_programs.description: The department provides quality education for students to design systems and devices in both technologies, conduct research, and pursue graduate studies. Students will do effective group work with leadership and entrepreneurship skills. Graduates are qualified to work in mobile operation companies, Internet service providing companies, TV/Radio Stations, Airports/Seaports, factories, and public/private institutions.
bachelor_programs.curriculum_note: Some courses marked with * are under Project ASEAN FACTORI 4.0 (609854-EPP-1-2019-1-FR-EPPKA2 CBHE-JP)
last_semester_optional_tracks: Coursework - for students who would like to conduct coursework and final project
Research Thesis - for outstanding students who would like to conduct their research project under the supervision of BE faculty members
Extended Training - for students who would like to participate in a long training period (for the entire semester) under a co-operative training program with companies or organizations
bachelor_programs.internship_requirement: Students are required to conduct a minimum 6 weeks internship at a standard company or organization during their vacation time of the third year or before.

curriculum.note: L = Lecture, P = Practice, S = Self Study. * = Under Project ASEAN FACTORI 4.0

master_programs.name: Biotechnology and Food Technology Master Program
master_programs.overview: The Biotechnology and Food Technology Master Program is designed for students who wish to study the application of biological, chemical, engineering, and physical principles to the conversion of raw agricultural products into processed foods suitable for human consumption, the nature of foods, the causes of deterioration, the principles underlying food processing, and the improvement of food quality and safety for public consumption.
master_programs.description: Prepares engineers for life-long achievement through education in Biotechnology, Food technology, and sustainable refining of natural products in Cambodia and development of communication, teamwork, leadership and entrepreneurship skills. Established through partnerships with three Swedish universities to create a stronger and more sustainable research group within the Faculty of Engineering.
master_programs.department_vision: To strengthen the research and research training capacity of RUPP in the areas of biotechnology, bioengineering and food science through a master-level research training program established in cooperation with Swedish partner universities.
master_programs.strategic_objective: Establish general competence in processing of biological raw materials to create valuable products from agriculture, forestry, and fishery, involving production of foods and materials as main products, as well as utilization of organic side streams.
program_tracks: Industrial Track - Thesis based professional development
benefits: Studying with foreign professors from Swedish partner universities
Full scholarship covering tuition fee and salary

curriculum.note: L = Lecture, P = Practice, S = Self Study

admission_requirements.bachelor_degree: Bachelor's degree (or equivalent) in a relevant field: Bioengineering, Biotechnology, Food Engineering, Biochemistry, Chemistry, Biology, Agro-industry, or Food Technology

english_proficiency.note: Applications without English proficiency evidence will be put on hold and will not be reviewed by the admission board

admission_requirements.bachelor_quality: Sufficient quality of the bachelor's degree

research_focus: Sustainable Processing of Biological Raw Materials

master_programs.name: Master of Science in Information Technology Engineering (MITE)
partnerships: Group T, International University College Leuven (Belgium)
master_programs.overview: Developed in response to the rapid advancement of ICT and growing demands for specialists in information technologies and telecommunications in Cambodia. Established in 2009, MITE upgrades skills and knowledge of students in information and telecommunication technologies to a specialist level for a variety of ICT careers.
master_programs.description: Prepares students to become specialists ranging from programmers/software developers, IT consultants, project managers in IT, network specialists to wireless and mobile engineers. Emphasizes sound knowledge of latest development and research trends in ICT around the world. Introduces students to international research projects and allows them to develop their own research projects to meet international academic standards.
master_programs.department_vision: MITE upgrades skills and knowledge of students in information and telecommunication technologies to specialist level. Students understand how research efforts in ICT are conducted by participating in international research projects and developing their own research to meet international academic standards.
program_features: Carefully-structured courses in first year to create solid foundation
Three special study areas allowing students to choose their preference
Second year focuses on research and specialization
Flexible study areas: Software Development, Network Engineering, or Multimedia Science

curriculum.note: L = Lecture, P = Practice, S = Self Study

specialization_tracks.name: Software Development Area
specialization_tracks.description: Education and research into the development of computer software for variety of purposes

specialization_tracks.name: Network Engineering Area
specialization_tracks.description: Preparation to become a professional network administrator or engineer in wired or wireless communications including Wireless LAN, WiMAX, cellular networks and satellite communications

specialization_tracks.name: Multimedia Science Area
specialization_tracks.description: Opportunities to learn and explore research into computer media, 3D modeling, computer graphics, multimedia presentation, and image processing and media design

cooperation_efforts: Cooperation with international universities in Japan, Europe and USA
Member of ASEAN University Network with MOUs with many international universities
Cooperation with private companies (local and international) for student experience and job opportunities
Fully equipped laboratories with high-tech computers and network equipment
Curriculum co-designed with university partners emphasizing research and professional skills

admission_requirements.english_test: Pass an English Test prepared by the Program
admission_requirements.bachelor_degree: Bachelor Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Management Information Systems, Electrical and Electronic Engineering or equivalent in relevant fields approved by academic board
admission_requirements.note: Applications without English proficiency evidence will be put on hold and will not be reviewed by the admission board
application_documents: Certified copy of Bachelor's Degree or equivalent
Valid ID card or copy of family book

general_info.admission_board: Admission committee judges every application on an individual basis
general_info.scholarship_availability: Full scholarships available for master programs - covers tuition fee and salary
general_info.internship_policy: All bachelor programs require minimum 6 weeks internship at a standard company or organization during vacation time of third year or before
general_info.honor_bachelor_note: Honor Bachelor programs are designed for exceptionally motivated and high-achieving students; thesis/senior project is mandatory
general_info.grading_assessment: Assessment performed at end of each semester; evaluation shown as GPA and Cumulative GPA on a 4.00 scale

about_us.vision: To pursue an excellence in teaching, research and community engagement in Engineering Education to address the challenges of ever-changing society and globalization.
mission: Deliver Comprehensive and Dynamic Education: Covers core engineering principles, advanced topics, and interdisciplinary studies to build a solid foundation of knowledge and skills.

Foster advancement in research: Promotes Innovation, collaboration and ethical practices; and to encourage faculty and students to think outside the box by integrating project-based learning, creative thinking methodologies, and opportunities to tackle real-world challenges; and Facilitates access to research projects, internships, and industry collaboration to give practical experience and exposure to cutting-edge technologies and practices.
Cultivate a Global Perspective: Prepare students for the global engineering landscape by offering international exchange programs and fostering collaborations with global institutions.
Enhance Soft Skills: Develop students communication, teamwork, leadership, and problem-solving abilities through group projects, presentations, and leadership roles within student organizations.
Encourage Lifelong Learning: Promote continuous education and professional development by encouraging students to stay abreast of emerging technologies, trends, and best practices in engineering.
Engage with the Community: Involve students in community service and outreach programs to help them understand the societal impact of their work and develop a sense of social responsibility.

dean_message.title: Dean, Faculty of Engineering
dean_message.message: The Faculty of Engineering, Royal University of Phnom Penh was set up on May, 07 2013 to respond to the increase demand of the industries and private sectors in the country for qualified engineers. The faculty, with four departments, offers now seven degrees programs. We thrive to bring international experiences and new innovative engineering education such as Conceive, Design, Implement, Operate (CDIO) framework to enhance existing programs and building new programs toward international level. We ventured into offering international programs in Food technology & Engineering, Data science & Engineering, 2+2 international program in Automation and Supply Chain System Engineering, and Environmental Engineering. Our engineering programs prepare our students equipped with positive mental attitude, intellectual, entrepreneurship and leadership capacity necessary to meet the challenges of the ever-changing society. With dedication, enthusiasm and hard work, the faculty has been able to produce graduates who are capable of addressing the requirement of the industries and private sectors. The faculty is dedicated to pursue and excellent teaching and research culture to deliver high quality education.

contact.location: Room 103, STEM Building, Royal University of Phnom Penh (Campus 1), Russian Federation Blvd (110), Phnom Penh
